data.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory + 2 overflow). Triage + decisions:
- [P5 f82524 — same-file siblings] FIXED: added WS3 depends_on WS2 so the Pass-2/3/4 extractions (all editing passes.py + workflow_ops.py) SERIALIZE WS1→WS2→WS3→WS4; they are sequential extractions on shared files, not parallelizable.
- [E3 fb843a / F1 fda3b1,f6248c — 'same verdicts' unspecified, effort-framed ACs] The behavioral-equivalence bar for each extraction = the produced plan_review_verdict PARTITION is equal (blocking/advisory/overflow/indeterminate/dropped finding ids + decisions) under a deterministic FakeRunner, pre- vs post-extraction. Each child's offline test asserts exactly that. (Outcome: 'the verdict partition is unchanged', not 'a test asserts…'.)
- [T4 fea58f — risky in-place rewire] The offline verdict-partition-equivalence test IS the parity safeguard for replacing plan-review's internal pass with the kernel call: the rewire is not merged unless the FakeRunner verdict is byte-equal. (Same discipline as the WS1 drift-probe parity gate in the superseded solid-timer-unison work.)
- [E2 ff3eee — live eval gating] The 'small gated live eval' reuses the EXISTING gated-eval CI pattern (the 'container fidelity (live — gated)' job): needs the [eval] extra + model credentials, SKIPS without them, and is OFF the blocking required-checks path. The offline FakeRunner eval is the CI-blocking one.
- [COH fdb3e6 — coach selection ordering] Clarified for WS3: order is (1) gate-on-surviving>0 (0 surviving → no coach call at all); else (2) deterministic applicability filter on each move's triggers/applies_when → the applicable subset; (3) the LLM picks among ONLY the applicable moves (move_id + bounded subject); (4) deterministic template render. The LLM never selects outside the applicable set.
- [E1 f06bc7] 'Pass-1 batching NOT shared' is an explicit NON-GOAL (already reusable via ProductionBatchRunner), not a deliverable — no AC needed. [P6] AC-quality noted. [T7 fd3209] docs are WS4's deliverable (kernel seam + enforcement doc). [G5 overflow] the 4 children + WS1-first ARE the decomposition / thin slice.
